raw.productSpecial:
Siyam World is a striking new vision of the Maldives’ rich natural wonders, a carefree playground with an exciting, diverse, and endless array of ‘never-seen-before’ experiences to bask in a WOW! Premium All-Inclusive island getaway that crosses cultures and borders.

Designed for fun-loving, open-minded couples, romance seekers, families or larger groups of friends, with the freedom to roam, socialise and engage in an island community. Siyam World is one of the largest islands in the Maldives to boast a resort and show-stopping residences - The Beach House Collection. 

The resort boasts an enticing variety of 18 accommodation categories of one-to six-bedroom villas, pavilions and The Beach House Collection residences, that span from 89 to 3,000 square metres.

 

Highlights:

1. Water Villa with Pool + Slide

2. Aqva Pool Bar 

3. Siyam Water World - Indian Oceans' Biggest Floating Water Park

4. 4 kms of white sandy beaches & 6 kms of house reef

5. 24/7 WOW! Premium all-inclusive privileges and benefits

**Food & Drinks - UNLIMITED DINING EXPERIENCES: 

BREAKFAST: Served at either Baraabaru or Tempo.

LUNCH: Available at Baraabaru, Shipwrecked and Jungali.

AFTERNOON SNACK: Available at Together, Shipwrecked, Mint and Jungali.

GRAND POP-UP BEACH BBQ: Wild array of beach bbq delicacies, Buffet style, happening once a week.

BARS: Enjoy unlimited creative cocktails, non-alcoholic drinks, soft drinks, coffee, tea, snacks and a variety of whiskeys, rums, gins and vodkas from well-known artisan distilleries at Jungali, Together and Mint.

24/7 KULHIVARU: Adults only Sports Bar - Your go-to snacks and drinks place - open 24/7.

MINI BAR: Stocked with soft drinks and snacks, as well as cold beers and great wine. Replenished once a day.

BEVERAGE INCLUSIONS:  Popular classic cocktails and signature concoctions, A curated wine list based on our love of authentic, simple and real tastes, Large selection of whiskeys, rums, vodkas and gins, Champagne available at all bars, Selection of beers and spirits from around the world, Home-pressed fresh juices, Classic soft drinks and sodas

**EXPERIENTIAL ADVENTURES - BYE BYE BOREDOM

EXCURSIONS & EXPEDITIONS: Take your pick between two of our dreamy daily ocean cruises and sail into the Noonu Atoll horizon with us: Lucky dolphin sunset cruise or Snorkeling safari.

SIYAM WATER WORLD: You have unlimited complimentary access to the biggest floating water park in Maldives located in the sunrise lagoon at Siyam World.

SPORTS & GAMES : For those looking at keeping fit, healthy and active during their holidays, we have a fun selection of complimentary activities for you, your family and friends.

ENTERTAINMENT: Live music and DJ, Local dance classes, Karaoke nights, Dance shows, Pool / beach parties, Foam parties

**AN AUTHENTIC MALDIVIAN EXPERIENCE

Gaadiyaa – we will surprise you on a daily basis at various locations on the island with our Gaadiyaa, the local food cart that serves up some delicious savory and sweet snacks, soft drinks, coconuts and tropical fruits.

6. For honeymooners

For honeymooners with 6 months valid marriage certificate are entitled for 1 bottle of sparkling wine and a fruit platter on arrival, complimentary candlelit dinner in one of our A la carte restaurant for the couple once during their stay, Special evening turn-down service with bed decoration and one celebratory honeymoon cake. Please do a remark when booking the product. Please bring a copy of your marriage certificate.

 

The above content is provided by the hotel for reference only, and will not be notified in case of change.

 

Room Type：

Water Villa with Pool + Slide: Three adults per room or two adults with 2 children per room.

Lagoon Villa with Pool + Slide: Three adults per room or two adults with 2 children per room.

Ocean Villa with Pool + Slide: Three adults per room.

itinerary.travelName: 馬爾地夫禧亞世嘉島 6日5晚

content.relatedName: 【MLEMLE6-2】馬累機場 - 禧亞世嘉度假村

decodedJson.name: 【MLEMLE6-2】馬累機場 - 禧亞世嘉度假村

decodedJson.description:
When you arrive at Velana International Airport, the resort staff will pick you up with a signboard with the hotel's name on it. Please present your passport and hotel check-in slip, and take a seaplane to the island after confirmation.
Reminder: Due to the limited operating time of water flights, please make sure you arrive at Male International Airport before 15:00 local time before you can take the seaplane to the island, thank you.

decodedJson.description:
Located on the island of Dhigurah, stretched across 0.9 mi of house reef in Noonu Atoll, Siyam World Maldives offers island escapism at a scale far beyond experience in a rich natural 54 hector island with an exciting collection of fun-filled adventures. Encircled by a pristine white beach, fringed with swaying palms and dense tropical vegetation, Siyam World Maldives can be reached by domestic flight to Maafaru airport from Velana International Airport. From Maafaru, it takes a short 15 minutes speedboat ride to reach the resort. Seaplane option also is available, which takes 45 minutes from Velana International Airport.

Siyam World Maldives offers fantastic island-wide dine-around opportunities with varied cuisines from pure vegetarian, raw, vegan, Italian, Indian and grill specialties. Also, four bars with unlimited beverages and the main bar open 24/7 offering a late-night light snack buffet.

decodedJson.name: 【ML-AIR】禧亞世嘉度假村 - 馬累機場

decodedJson.description:
After check-out, the hotel will arrange for you to leave the island today. Please follow the arrangement of the hotel staff, gather at the designated location of the hotel on time, and return to Velana International Airport by seaplane.

Upon arrival at Male Airport, check-in by yourself.

cost.includesHtml:
1. Five night accommodation on Premium 24hrs WOW! Room type: Water Villa with Pool + Slide.

2. Round trip seaplane transfers.
cost.includesText:
1. Five night accommodation on Premium 24hrs WOW! Room type: Water Villa with Pool + Slide.

2. Round trip seaplane transfers.
cost.excludesHtml:
1. Flight tickets, taxes, visa fees and passport processing fees. 

2. Person fees such as laundry, haircut, telephone, fax, pay TV, drinks, tips and any additional expenses inside the hotel. 

3. Any personal expenses that are not listed in the Fees Included.
cost.excludesText:
1. Flight tickets, taxes, visa fees and passport processing fees. 

2. Person fees such as laundry, haircut, telephone, fax, pay TV, drinks, tips and any additional expenses inside the hotel. 

3. Any personal expenses that are not listed in the Fees Included.

html.primary:
1. Itinerary Cancellation Policy:

Prior to departure Penalty charge (% of total tour fee)
Less than 10 days prior to departure date (EST)  100%
Less than 17 days but 11 days prior to departure date (EST) 50%
Less than 33 days but 18 days prior to departure date (EST) 25%
34 days prior to departure date (EST) 0

Notes:

(1) Paid tour fees will NOT be refunded in the case of no-shows on the day of departure.

(2)  If a guest quits during the trip or fails to participate in any itinerary due to personal reasons (no travel documents, late arrival, illness, accident, etc.), the paid tour fare will not be refunded, and no other services will be compensated.

(3)  Additional hotel bookings before or after the tour as well as other services are subject to hotel cancellation policy to determine if a refund is possible.

 

2. In the event of weather, war, strikes and other force majeure that make it impossible to tour, Jupiter Legend will try its best to protect the safety of guests and their property. Jupiter Legend reserves the right to cancel or adjust part or all of the itinerary, and will not bear any direct or indirect responsibility caused by force majeure. The paid tour fee is non-refundable.

html.primary:
1. The Maldives is a Muslim country. It is forbidden to bring pork and alcohol into the Maldives, and the customs may confiscate them all. At the same time, it is not allowed to take seawater, debris from shipwrecks, shells from the island (not purchasable items) or coral out of the Maldives.
2. Do not offend local customs when traveling, especially in the capital Male, the dress should not be too casual, men should not be bare-chested, women should not wear low-cut tops, it is best to wear long skirts, trousers and other pants that can cover the legs. You cannot wear shoes when entering the mosque, and you must take off your shoes and place them neatly outside. Men are not allowed to wear shorts, women are not allowed to wear suspender skirts and short skirts, and hats and umbrellas should also be put away. Island resorts are relatively loose, but it is best not to wear revealing clothes when entering public places such as dining areas and bars.
3. When taking pictures on the residential islands of the Maldives, you need to pay attention to avoiding the locals as much as possible. If you need to photograph local people, you must obtain the consent of the person concerned. In addition, when visiting Male, some places cannot be photographed, such as the location of the Sea Cliff Guard in Male. Therefore, before taking pictures, be sure to see the surrounding environment or ask clearly, otherwise you may be fined.
4. It is strictly forbidden to fish without permission and damage corals. It is strictly forbidden for tourists to collect sand or shells out of the country without permission. If they are found, they will be fined. If necessary, they can go to the local shopping mall to buy them and ask whether they can be taken out of the country. Additionally, it is illegal to purchase items made from sea turtles.
5. The shoals around the small islands of the Maldives seem calm, but in fact there are undercurrents and complex ecological environments. For safety reasons, please understand the surrounding waters in advance, identify the characteristics of common marine life, safety signs and the location of lifeguards. Before going into the water, please make sufficient preparations on the shore, bring the necessary equipment, and be sure to carry out various water activities with someone accompanying you. Please pay attention to the ebb and flow of the tide when snorkeling. The time of ebb and flow varies according to the season. Please confirm with the hotel on the day for specific information. For your safety, please be sure to ask the island lifeguards, diving instructors and other professionals or the front desk of the hotel to learn about various relevant precautions before water activities on the day, and do not rush into the water or play in the water.
6. The direct sunlight in the Maldives is very strong. Please be sure to pay attention to sun protection. Wear sunscreen and a sun hat before going out to avoid direct sunlight and burn your skin. If you like snorkeling, you can prepare wetsuits, masks, and underwater cameras in advance to record the colorful underwater world of Maldives. It is recommended to bring mosquito repellent if necessary.
7. The official language of the Maldives is Divehi, which is an ancient Ceylon language from Sri Lanka, and later mixed with English, Arabic, Indian and other elements. At present, English is widely used as a second language. In the urban area of Malé on the airport island and the resorts on the islands, educated people can speak English very well, but it is more difficult to use English on the aboriginal islands.
8. Rufiyya (Rafia) is used on Malé Island, and US dollars can also be used. It is recommended that guests exchange a small amount of US dollars in advance for payment of tips.
9. It is recommended to take the initiative to pay the tip to the waiter after enjoying the service. You can put it next to the pillow after the waiter helps you carry your luggage; before the waiter cleans the room; After collecting the service fee (usually 10%), there is no need to tip.

shopify_mapping.title: Siyam World Maldives 6-Day 5-Night Tour
shopify_mapping.bodyHtml:
Highlights

Siyam World is a striking new vision of the Maldives’ rich natural wonders, a carefree playground with an exciting, diverse, and endless array of ‘never-seen-before’ experiences to bask in a WOW! Premium All-Inclusive island getaway that crosses cultures and borders.

Designed for fun-loving, open-minded couples, romance seekers, families or larger groups of friends, with the freedom to roam, socialise and engage in an island community. Siyam World is one of the largest islands in the Maldives to boast a resort and show-stopping residences - The Beach House Collection. 

The resort boasts an enticing variety of 18 accommodation categories of one-to six-bedroom villas, pavilions and The Beach House Collection residences, that span from 89 to 3,000 square metres.

 

Highlights:

1. Water Villa with Pool + Slide

2. Aqva Pool Bar 

3. Siyam Water World - Indian Oceans' Biggest Floating Water Park

4. 4 kms of white sandy beaches & 6 kms of house reef

5. 24/7 WOW! Premium all-inclusive privileges and benefits

**Food & Drinks - UNLIMITED DINING EXPERIENCES: 

BREAKFAST: Served at either Baraabaru or Tempo.

LUNCH: Available at Baraabaru, Shipwrecked and Jungali.

AFTERNOON SNACK: Available at Together, Shipwrecked, Mint and Jungali.

GRAND POP-UP BEACH BBQ: Wild array of beach bbq delicacies, Buffet style, happening once a week.

BARS: Enjoy unlimited creative cocktails, non-alcoholic drinks, soft drinks, coffee, tea, snacks and a variety of whiskeys, rums, gins and vodkas from well-known artisan distilleries at Jungali, Together and Mint.

24/7 KULHIVARU: Adults only Sports Bar - Your go-to snacks and drinks place - open 24/7.

MINI BAR: Stocked with soft drinks and snacks, as well as cold beers and great wine. Replenished once a day.

BEVERAGE INCLUSIONS:  Popular classic cocktails and signature concoctions, A curated wine list based on our love of authentic, simple and real tastes, Large selection of whiskeys, rums, vodkas and gins, Champagne available at all bars, Selection of beers and spirits from around the world, Home-pressed fresh juices, Classic soft drinks and sodas

**EXPERIENTIAL ADVENTURES - BYE BYE BOREDOM

EXCURSIONS & EXPEDITIONS: Take your pick between two of our dreamy daily ocean cruises and sail into the Noonu Atoll horizon with us: Lucky dolphin sunset cruise or Snorkeling safari.

SIYAM WATER WORLD: You have unlimited complimentary access to the biggest floating water park in Maldives located in the sunrise lagoon at Siyam World.

SPORTS & GAMES : For those looking at keeping fit, healthy and active during their holidays, we have a fun selection of complimentary activities for you, your family and friends.

ENTERTAINMENT: Live music and DJ, Local dance classes, Karaoke nights, Dance shows, Pool / beach parties, Foam parties

**AN AUTHENTIC MALDIVIAN EXPERIENCE

Gaadiyaa – we will surprise you on a daily basis at various locations on the island with our Gaadiyaa, the local food cart that serves up some delicious savory and sweet snacks, soft drinks, coconuts and tropical fruits.

6. For honeymooners

For honeymooners with 6 months valid marriage certificate are entitled for 1 bottle of sparkling wine and a fruit platter on arrival, complimentary candlelit dinner in one of our A la carte restaurant for the couple once during their stay, Special evening turn-down service with bed decoration and one celebratory honeymoon cake. Please do a remark when booking the product. Please bring a copy of your marriage certificate.

 

The above content is provided by the hotel for reference only, and will not be notified in case of change.

 

Room Type：

Water Villa with Pool + Slide: Three adults per room or two adults with 2 children per room.

Lagoon Villa with Pool + Slide: Three adults per room or two adults with 2 children per room.

Ocean Villa with Pool + Slide: Three adults per room.

 

Departure Date

Daily Departure.
During the peak season, the price of the tour will increase, please click the specific date to check the final price.
Minimum 2 people, requires secondary confirmation.

Cost Includes

1. Five night accommodation on Premium 24hrs WOW! Room type: Water Villa with Pool + Slide.

2. Round trip seaplane transfers.

Cost Excludes

1. Flight tickets, taxes, visa fees and passport processing fees. 

2. Person fees such as laundry, haircut, telephone, fax, pay TV, drinks, tips and any additional expenses inside the hotel. 

3. Any personal expenses that are not listed in the Fees Included.

Cancellation Policy

1. Itinerary Cancellation Policy:

Prior to departure Penalty charge (% of total tour fee)
Less than 10 days prior to departure date (EST)  100%
Less than 17 days but 11 days prior to departure date (EST) 50%
Less than 33 days but 18 days prior to departure date (EST) 25%
34 days prior to departure date (EST) 0

Notes:

(1) Paid tour fees will NOT be refunded in the case of no-shows on the day of departure.

(2)  If a guest quits during the trip or fails to participate in any itinerary due to personal reasons (no travel documents, late arrival, illness, accident, etc.), the paid tour fare will not be refunded, and no other services will be compensated.

(3)  Additional hotel bookings before or after the tour as well as other services are subject to hotel cancellation policy to determine if a refund is possible.

 

2. In the event of weather, war, strikes and other force majeure that make it impossible to tour, Jupiter Legend will try its best to protect the safety of guests and their property. Jupiter Legend reserves the right to cancel or adjust part or all of the itinerary, and will not bear any direct or indirect responsibility caused by force majeure. The paid tour fee is non-refundable.

Know Before You Book

1. The Maldives is a Muslim country. It is forbidden to bring pork and alcohol into the Maldives, and the customs may confiscate them all. At the same time, it is not allowed to take seawater, debris from shipwrecks, shells from the island (not purchasable items) or coral out of the Maldives.
2. Do not offend local customs when traveling, especially in the capital Male, the dress should not be too casual, men should not be bare-chested, women should not wear low-cut tops, it is best to wear long skirts, trousers and other pants that can cover the legs. You cannot wear shoes when entering the mosque, and you must take off your shoes and place them neatly outside. Men are not allowed to wear shorts, women are not allowed to wear suspender skirts and short skirts, and hats and umbrellas should also be put away. Island resorts are relatively loose, but it is best not to wear revealing clothes when entering public places such as dining areas and bars.
3. When taking pictures on the residential islands of the Maldives, you need to pay attention to avoiding the locals as much as possible. If you need to photograph local people, you must obtain the consent of the person concerned. In addition, when visiting Male, some places cannot be photographed, such as the location of the Sea Cliff Guard in Male. Therefore, before taking pictures, be sure to see the surrounding environment or ask clearly, otherwise you may be fined.
4. It is strictly forbidden to fish without permission and damage corals. It is strictly forbidden for tourists to collect sand or shells out of the country without permission. If they are found, they will be fined. If necessary, they can go to the local shopping mall to buy them and ask whether they can be taken out of the country. Additionally, it is illegal to purchase items made from sea turtles.
5. The shoals around the small islands of the Maldives seem calm, but in fact there are undercurrents and complex ecological environments. For safety reasons, please understand the surrounding waters in advance, identify the characteristics of common marine life, safety signs and the location of lifeguards. Before going into the water, please make sufficient preparations on the shore, bring the necessary equipment, and be sure to carry out various water activities with someone accompanying you. Please pay attention to the ebb and flow of the tide when snorkeling. The time of ebb and flow varies according to the season. Please confirm with the hotel on the day for specific information. For your safety, please be sure to ask the island lifeguards, diving instructors and other professionals or the front desk of the hotel to learn about various relevant precautions before water activities on the day, and do not rush into the water or play in the water.
6. The direct sunlight in the Maldives is very strong. Please be sure to pay attention to sun protection. Wear sunscreen and a sun hat before going out to avoid direct sunlight and burn your skin. If you like snorkeling, you can prepare wetsuits, masks, and underwater cameras in advance to record the colorful underwater world of Maldives. It is recommended to bring mosquito repellent if necessary.
7. The official language of the Maldives is Divehi, which is an ancient Ceylon language from Sri Lanka, and later mixed with English, Arabic, Indian and other elements. At present, English is widely used as a second language. In the urban area of Malé on the airport island and the resorts on the islands, educated people can speak English very well, but it is more difficult to use English on the aboriginal islands.
8. Rufiyya (Rafia) is used on Malé Island, and US dollars can also be used. It is recommended that guests exchange a small amount of US dollars in advance for payment of tips.
9. It is recommended to take the initiative to pay the tip to the waiter after enjoying the service. You can put it next to the pillow after the waiter helps you carry your luggage; before the waiter cleans the room; After collecting the service fee (usually 10%), there is no need to tip.

noticeInfo.vluesTip1:
1. Itinerary Cancellation Policy:

Prior to departure Penalty charge (% of total tour fee)
Less than 10 days prior to departure date (EST)  100%
Less than 17 days but 11 days prior to departure date (EST) 50%
Less than 33 days but 18 days prior to departure date (EST) 25%
34 days prior to departure date (EST) 0

Notes:

(1) Paid tour fees will NOT be refunded in the case of no-shows on the day of departure.

(2)  If a guest quits during the trip or fails to participate in any itinerary due to personal reasons (no travel documents, late arrival, illness, accident, etc.), the paid tour fare will not be refunded, and no other services will be compensated.

(3)  Additional hotel bookings before or after the tour as well as other services are subject to hotel cancellation policy to determine if a refund is possible.

 

2. In the event of weather, war, strikes and other force majeure that make it impossible to tour, Jupiter Legend will try its best to protect the safety of guests and their property. Jupiter Legend reserves the right to cancel or adjust part or all of the itinerary, and will not bear any direct or indirect responsibility caused by force majeure. The paid tour fee is non-refundable.

1. The Maldives is a Muslim country. It is forbidden to bring pork and alcohol into the Maldives, and the customs may confiscate them all. At the same time, it is not allowed to take seawater, debris from shipwrecks, shells from the island (not purchasable items) or coral out of the Maldives.
2. Do not offend local customs when traveling, especially in the capital Male, the dress should not be too casual, men should not be bare-chested, women should not wear low-cut tops, it is best to wear long skirts, trousers and other pants that can cover the legs. You cannot wear shoes when entering the mosque, and you must take off your shoes and place them neatly outside. Men are not allowed to wear shorts, women are not allowed to wear suspender skirts and short skirts, and hats and umbrellas should also be put away. Island resorts are relatively loose, but it is best not to wear revealing clothes when entering public places such as dining areas and bars.
3. When taking pictures on the residential islands of the Maldives, you need to pay attention to avoiding the locals as much as possible. If you need to photograph local people, you must obtain the consent of the person concerned. In addition, when visiting Male, some places cannot be photographed, such as the location of the Sea Cliff Guard in Male. Therefore, before taking pictures, be sure to see the surrounding environment or ask clearly, otherwise you may be fined.
4. It is strictly forbidden to fish without permission and damage corals. It is strictly forbidden for tourists to collect sand or shells out of the country without permission. If they are found, they will be fined. If necessary, they can go to the local shopping mall to buy them and ask whether they can be taken out of the country. Additionally, it is illegal to purchase items made from sea turtles.
5. The shoals around the small islands of the Maldives seem calm, but in fact there are undercurrents and complex ecological environments. For safety reasons, please understand the surrounding waters in advance, identify the characteristics of common marine life, safety signs and the location of lifeguards. Before going into the water, please make sufficient preparations on the shore, bring the necessary equipment, and be sure to carry out various water activities with someone accompanying you. Please pay attention to the ebb and flow of the tide when snorkeling. The time of ebb and flow varies according to the season. Please confirm with the hotel on the day for specific information. For your safety, please be sure to ask the island lifeguards, diving instructors and other professionals or the front desk of the hotel to learn about various relevant precautions before water activities on the day, and do not rush into the water or play in the water.
6. The direct sunlight in the Maldives is very strong. Please be sure to pay attention to sun protection. Wear sunscreen and a sun hat before going out to avoid direct sunlight and burn your skin. If you like snorkeling, you can prepare wetsuits, masks, and underwater cameras in advance to record the colorful underwater world of Maldives. It is recommended to bring mosquito repellent if necessary.
7. The official language of the Maldives is Divehi, which is an ancient Ceylon language from Sri Lanka, and later mixed with English, Arabic, Indian and other elements. At present, English is widely used as a second language. In the urban area of Malé on the airport island and the resorts on the islands, educated people can speak English very well, but it is more difficult to use English on the aboriginal islands.
8. Rufiyya (Rafia) is used on Malé Island, and US dollars can also be used. It is recommended that guests exchange a small amount of US dollars in advance for payment of tips.
9. It is recommended to take the initiative to pay the tip to the waiter after enjoying the service. You can put it next to the pillow after the waiter helps you carry your luggage; before the waiter cleans the room; After collecting the service fee (usually 10%), there is no need to tip.

1. Five night accommodation on Premium 24hrs WOW! Room type: Water Villa with Pool + Slide.

2. Round trip seaplane transfers.

2. Person fees such as laundry, haircut, telephone, fax, pay TV, drinks, tips and any additional expenses inside the hotel. 

3. Any personal expenses that are not listed in the Fees Included.
